name: schedule
description: Create and manage scheduled tasks. An agent can schedule a one-off or recurring (cron) task that sends a prompt back to itself or runs a shell command on the gateway host. The agent that creates the schedule is always the one that receives the trigger — cross-agent scheduling is not supported. Exec actions require allowExec: true in the plugin config.
commands: test    <id>  — trigger immediately regardless of nextRun

storagePath.description: Directory where schedule files are stored. Defaults to ~/.beige/plugins/schedule. Tilde is expanded.

tickInterval.description: How often (in seconds) the background loop checks for due schedules. Default: 15.

allowExec.description: Whether agents may create exec-type schedules (shell commands on the gateway host). Default: true. Agents are sandboxed, so exec runs on the gateway side of the trust boundary — disable explicitly if needed.

maxSchedulesPerAgent.description: Maximum number of active (non-completed, non-cancelled) schedules per agent. Default: 20.
